patch_description:
This patch implements a crossover-inspired hybrid ANSATZ_SPEC that merges the strongest design choices from the two given seeds while preserving a compact parameter count. The key ideas:
- Maintain the symmetric pre- and post-entanglement RZ layers and the RY rotations with per-group parameter sharing.
- Keep CZ ring entanglement on the outer perimeter as in both seeds (depth/efficiency benefit).
- Introduce hub entanglement between the center qubit (8) and edge qubits (1,3,5,7) using CRZ and CRX gates, but share a single hub_angle parameter across all hub gates to keep N_PARAMS_PER_BLOCK at 10 (thus N_PARAMS = 60 with N_UPLOADS=3 and N_REPEATS=2). This preserves parameter efficiency while benefiting from increased expressivity.
- This design aligns with the best-performing pattern observed (60 params per block) and potentially improves validation/test metrics without changing the fixed training loop, data construction, measurements, l, p, or feature map.

The program preserves all inputs/outputs, only modifying the ANSATZ_SPEC block (EVOLVE-BLOCK) to implement the hybrid scheme.
